faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about SnapOtter.

tool.selfHosted.title: Self-hosted. Your files never leave your network.
tool.selfHosted.aiTitle: AI that runs on your hardware. No cloud APIs, no usage limits.
tool.selfHosted.body: SnapOtter runs entirely on your own infrastructure. Files processed with {tool} are never uploaded to third-party servers. Deploy a single Docker container and process files with full privacy, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Open source under AGPL-3.0.
tool.selfHosted.aiBody: Unlike cloud AI services, SnapOtter's {tool} runs the ML model directly on your server. Your files are processed locally with no data sent to external APIs. No per-file fees, no rate limits, no privacy concerns. Deploy once with Docker and use it as much as you need.
tool.ctaBody: Deploy SnapOtter in under a minute. All {count} tools included. Open source and free forever.
toolsIndex.metaDescription: Browse all {count} self-hosted file processing tools: resize, compress, convert, remove backgrounds, upscale with AI, add watermarks, generate QR codes, and more. Free, open source, runs on your hardware.
toolsIndex.title: All {count} tools
toolsIndex.subtitle: Every tool runs 100% on your hardware. No cloud uploads, no usage limits, no watermarks. Deploy once with Docker and use them all.
toolsIndex.empty: No tools match your search. Try a different term or modality.
toolsIndex.ctaTitle: Deploy all {count} tools with one command
toolsIndex.ctaBody: One Docker Compose stack. No cloud services. Open source and free forever.
section.title: {section} Tools
alt.runOnYourServer: Run it on your own server
altIndex.metaTitle: Open-Source, Self-Hosted Alternatives to Cloud File Tools | SnapOtter
altIndex.metaDescription: Open-source, self-hosted alternatives to TinyWow, Smallpdf, Adobe Acrobat online, FreeConvert, Convertio, CloudConvert, Otter.ai, and more. One stack for image, video, audio, PDF, and files.
altIndex.title: Open-source alternatives to cloud file tools
altIndex.subtitle: Most online file tools upload your work to someone else's server. SnapOtter runs the same kinds of tools on yours: 241 of them across image, video, audio, PDF, and files, in one self-hosted stack.
altIndex.methodology.title: Comparisons that age gracefully
altIndex.methodology.dated.title: Dated reviews
altIndex.methodology.dated.body: Every comparison page shows when the vendor pages were last checked, so stale claims are easier to spot.
altIndex.methodology.sources.title: Public sources
altIndex.methodology.sources.body: Source links sit next to the comparison instead of being buried in a private note.
altIndex.methodology.criteria.title: Stable criteria
altIndex.methodology.criteria.body: The core comparison is deployment model, file location, auditability, and workflow fit, not fragile point-in-time feature trivia.
altIndex.why.title: Why teams pick the self-hosted route
altIndex.why.yours.title: Your files stay yours
altIndex.why.yours.body: Processing happens on hardware you control. Nothing is uploaded to a third-party service, which matters when the file is a contract, a medical scan, or unreleased work.
altIndex.why.noQuotas.title: No quotas or per-file fees
altIndex.why.noQuotas.body: The only limit is your machine and your configuration. No third-party per-conversion billing for internal jobs.
altIndex.why.oneStack.title: One stack, five file types
altIndex.why.oneStack.body: Image, video, audio, PDF, and files in a single deployment, instead of a separate account for each job.
altIndex.why.openSource.title: Open source, auditable
altIndex.why.openSource.body: AGPLv3. Your team can read every line, run it air-gapped, and keep it as long as you like.
altIndex.cta.body: Open source, free forever, and your files never leave your network.
altSlug.lastReviewed: Last reviewed {date}. Comparison uses publicly available vendor pages and focuses on deployment model, file location, auditability, and workflow fit. Vendor features and plans change; check current docs before deciding. SnapOtter is not affiliated with {competitor}.
altSlug.breadthTitle: One stack, all five file types
altSlug.ctaBody: 200+ tools across image, video, audio, PDF, and files. Open source, free forever, and your files never leave your network.
faq.q1.q: Are my files safe and private?
faq.q1.a: All processing happens on your own server. Your files are never sent to any external service. SnapOtter runs entirely on your infrastructure.
faq.q2.a: Yes. SnapOtter is open source under AGPL-3.0 with no hidden fees, no subscription plans, no usage limits, and no premium-only features. A commercial license is available for organizations that need proprietary use.
faq.q3.q: Do I need an internet connection?
faq.q3.a: No. SnapOtter runs fully offline once deployed. No external API calls are made. It works in completely air-gapped environments.
faq.q4.q: Are there any file size or usage limitations?
faq.q4.a: No artificial limits. You can process as many files as you want, at any size. The only constraint is your server's hardware resources.
faq.q5.a: SnapOtter includes 19 local AI tools for background removal, upscaling, OCR, transcription, face detection, colorization, noise removal, photo restoration, and more. All models run on your hardware.
faq.q6.a: Anonymous product analytics is on by default and never includes your files or their contents. Turn it off any time with a one-click admin toggle in Settings > System > Privacy.
faq.q7.q: Can I use SnapOtter in my company?
faq.q7.a: Yes. Under AGPL-3.0, you can use it freely as long as you comply with the license terms. For proprietary or closed-source use, a commercial license is available.
faq.q8.a: Pull the latest Docker image and restart the container. Your data persists in the mounted volume.

privacy.metaDescription: SnapOtter is self-hosted software. We do not process, store, or have access to your files or data. Learn about our privacy practices.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.controller.title: Data Controller
privacy.controller.body: SnapOtter is a product of Chocolate Wafers General Trading Pte. Ltd. (UEN 202527149C), registered in Singapore. References to "we", "us", or "our" in this policy refer to Chocolate Wafers General Trading Pte. Ltd.
privacy.overview.title: Overview
privacy.overview.body: SnapOtter is self-hosted software. We do not process, store, or have access to your files or data. This privacy policy covers the snapotter.com website and optional analytics within the software.
privacy.website.title: Website (snapotter.com)
privacy.website.item1: We do not use tracking cookies.
privacy.website.item2: We use Cloudflare Web Analytics for anonymous, aggregate traffic metrics (page views, visits, referrers). It uses no cookies, collects no personal data, and does not track individual users across sites.
privacy.website.item3: Contact form submissions are processed via Formspree. Their privacy policy applies to that data.
privacy.selfHosted.title: Self-Hosted Software
privacy.selfHosted.item1: All file processing happens entirely on your server.
privacy.selfHosted.item2: Your files are never sent anywhere.
privacy.selfHosted.item3: No personal data, file contents, or file names leave your network.
privacy.analytics.title: Analytics
privacy.analytics.item1: Anonymous product analytics (tool usage patterns, error reports) help improve the software. Your files, file names, and personal data are never included.
privacy.analytics.item2: It is on by default. An administrator can turn it off any time with a one-click toggle under Settings > System > Privacy. No rebuild is required, and it stops immediately for the whole instance.
privacy.analytics.item3.pre: To build an image that can never emit analytics, set the
privacy.analytics.item3.post: build arg as a compile-time hard-off.
privacy.analytics.item4.pre: Optional in-app feedback is sent only when a user submits it, and only when analytics is enabled. Feedback appears in PostHog as
privacy.analytics.item4.post: Contact details are included only after explicit contact consent. Files, file names, upload paths, private outputs, and raw error logs are not included.
privacy.contactForm.title: Contact Form
privacy.contactForm.item1: When you submit the contact form, your name, email, company, and message are sent to us via Formspree.
privacy.contactForm.item2: We use this information only to respond to your inquiry.
privacy.contactForm.item3: We do not sell or share your contact information.
privacy.openSource.title: Open Source
privacy.openSource.body: SnapOtter is open source. You can inspect the entire codebase to verify our privacy practices.
privacy.changes.title: Changes
privacy.changes.body: We may update this policy. Changes will be posted on this page.
privacy.contact.title: Contact
privacy.contact.pre: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact us at

enterprise.metaDescription: Enterprise-grade self-hosted file processing. SAML SSO, audit logging, per-tool permissions, multi-tenancy. Deploy on your infrastructure.
enterprise.hero.subtitle: SAML SSO. Audit logging. Per-tool permissions. Multi-tenancy. All self-hosted, all open source at its core.
enterprise.why.title: Why enterprises choose self-hosted.
enterprise.why.subtitle: Keep full control over your data, your costs, and your compliance posture.
enterprise.why.compliance.title: Compliance by Architecture
enterprise.why.compliance.description: GDPR, HIPAA, CCPA. Your files never leave your network. No data processing agreements needed with third parties.
enterprise.why.zeroDeps.title: Zero External Dependencies
enterprise.why.zeroDeps.description: No API keys, no cloud services, no internet required. Works in air-gapped environments and classified networks.
enterprise.why.cost.title: Total Cost Control
enterprise.why.cost.description: No per-user pricing, no per-file charges. Deploy once, use unlimited. Your hardware, your capacity.
enterprise.features.title: Every feature your team needs.
enterprise.features.subtitle: Security, compliance, and deployment capabilities built for enterprise requirements.
enterprise.identity.title: Single sign-on for your entire team
enterprise.identity.body: SAML 2.0 and OIDC integration with any identity provider. Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, and more. SCIM provisioning for automated user lifecycle management. Enforce MFA across your organization.
enterprise.permissions.title: Granular control. Complete visibility.
enterprise.permissions.body: Role-based access control with admin, editor, user, and custom roles. Per-tool permissions let you restrict which tools each team can access. Every action is logged with full audit trails exportable for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and compliance reporting.
enterprise.storage.title: Your storage. Your rules.
enterprise.storage.body: Use local storage or connect any S3-compatible backend: AWS S3, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, Google Cloud Storage. Multi-tenancy isolates teams with separate workspaces, configurations, and storage paths.
enterprise.deployment.title: Deploy anywhere. Scale as needed.
enterprise.deployment.body: One Docker Compose stack: app, PostgreSQL, and Redis, with FFmpeg, LibreOffice, and local AI models in the app image. Deploy on Kubernetes with Helm charts, bare metal, or cloud VMs. ARM and x86 architectures. Air-gapped network support.
enterprise.comparison.title: SnapOtter vs. Cloud Processing
enterprise.comparison.subtitle: See why teams choose self-hosted file processing over cloud alternatives.

home.enterprise.title: Built for enterprise deployment.
home.enterprise.subtitle: Every identity, access, and compliance control your security team expects, running entirely on infrastructure you own.
home.enterprise.feature.sso.title: SAML SSO
home.enterprise.feature.sso.description: Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, or any SAML 2.0 / OIDC identity provider.
home.enterprise.feature.scim.title: SCIM Provisioning
home.enterprise.feature.scim.description: Automated user provisioning and deprovisioning synced from your identity provider.
home.enterprise.feature.mfa.title: Multi-Factor Auth
home.enterprise.feature.mfa.description: Enforce TOTP-based MFA across your organization. Protect every account.
home.enterprise.feature.permissions.desc: Granular role-based access control. Admin, editor, user, and custom roles.
home.enterprise.feature.multiTenancy.des: Isolated workspaces per team or department with separate configurations.
home.enterprise.feature.audit.title: Audit Export
home.enterprise.feature.audit.descriptio: Every action logged. Export immutable audit trails for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and compliance reporting.
home.enterprise.feature.s3.title: S3-Compatible Storage
home.enterprise.feature.s3.description: AWS S3, MinIO, or Cloudflare R2 for persistent, scalable file storage.
home.enterprise.feature.webhooks.title: Webhooks
home.enterprise.feature.webhooks.descrip: Real-time notifications when processing completes. Integrate with any internal workflow.
home.enterprise.ctaBody: Talk to our team about enterprise licensing and deployment.
home.features.title: Built for regulated environments.
home.features.subtitle: Your files stay on your infrastructure. No third-party processing, no data transfer risk.
home.features.f1.title: Compliant by architecture
home.features.f1.body: No vendor DPAs. No data processing agreements. No third-party risk assessments. Files are processed on your infrastructure and never transmitted to external services. GDPR, HIPAA, and CCPA compliant from day one.
home.features.f2.title: Integrate with your existing workflows
home.features.f2.body: Full REST API with OpenAPI documentation for every tool. Chain tools into automated pipelines. Batch process thousands of files. Trigger via webhooks and integrate with your internal systems.
home.features.f2.codeComment1: # Resize an image via the API
home.features.f3.title: AI processing without external APIs
home.features.f3.body: 19 local AI tools for OCR, transcription, background removal, upscaling, and more. No external API keys, no per-request charges, no data sent to third-party AI providers. Runs on CPU or NVIDIA CUDA within your own infrastructure.
home.features.f4.title: Fits your infrastructure
home.features.f4.body: One Docker Compose stack: app, Postgres, and Redis. Deploy on Kubernetes, bare metal, or cloud VMs. ARM and x86 architectures. Works in air-gapped and classified environments with zero internet dependency.
home.pricing.title: Free for everyone. Enterprise when you need it.
home.pricing.subtitle: Start with the full open source edition. Upgrade when your team needs enterprise controls.
home.pricing.oss.name: Open Source
home.pricing.oss.description: Everything you need for file processing. No limits, no trials, no credit card.
home.pricing.oss.feature.api: Full REST API with OpenAPI docs
home.pricing.oss.feature.oidc: OIDC / SSO login (Google, Okta, any provider)
home.pricing.enterprise.name: Enterprise
home.pricing.enterprise.description: Commercial license, enterprise security, priority support, and deployment assistance.
home.openSource.title: Open source. Fully auditable.
home.openSource.subtitle: AGPL-3.0 licensed. Your security team can inspect every line. No black boxes, no vendor lock-in.
